regions.us-ports: Today in Charleston, south winds at 7 knots bring clear skies and a 0% chance of rain, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions with no weather disruptions expected. Over the next 24-48 hours, winds will increase to southwest at 19 knots with a 54% chance of drizzle, which may affect port operations and shore excursions in Charleston as well as nearby ports including Savannah and Jacksonville. By day three, east-northeast winds at 10 knots will bring overcast skies and a 20% rain chance, presenting minimal impact for Norfolk, Port Canaveral, and Miami. Cruise vessels should monitor these evolving wind shifts and rain probabilities to adjust scheduling and tender operations accordingly. No significant changes beyond 48 hours are currently forecast in the US Ports region.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, conditions across the Bahamas and Central Caribbean are generally favorable with light winds and minimal rain. At Nassau, wind is from the NNW at 2 knots with clear skies and only a 2% chance of rain, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions. Over in Freeport, Bimini, Key West, and Grand Cayman, similarly light winds and low rain probabilities will keep port operations steady. For the next 24 to 48 hours, rain showers with an 11% chance are forecast primarily for Nassau, Freeport, and Bimini, accompanied by SSW winds at 8 knots, though this rain probability remains too low to affect operations. Beyond 48 hours, skies will remain mostly overcast with SSE winds increasing to 10 knots in Nassau and surrounding ports, along with rain chances rising slightly to 8%, but no significant impacts to cruise schedules or port activity are expected.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today in San Juan we have light SSE winds at 5 knots and clear skies with zero percent chance of rain, supporting smooth port operations and shore excursions across all Eastern Caribbean ports. Over the next 24 to 48 hours expect ESE winds increasing to 13 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 4 percent rain chance, conditions that will extend to St. Thomas, St. Croix, and Antigua without any rain impact on cruise activities. By Day 2, easterly winds will pick up to 14 knots accompanied by drizzle and a 14 percent rain probability, especially around St. Maarten and St. Kitts, though this low rain chance does not pose operational concerns. Beyond 48 hours, winds will continue from the east at 16 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 7 percent rain chance, maintaining generally favorable conditions for embarkation, disembarkation, and excursions throughout the Eastern Caribbean region.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, Cozumel is experiencing southeast winds at 8 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 0% rain chance, allowing normal port operations and shore excursions with no weather-related impacts. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, southeast winds will remain steady near 11 knots as rain showers with a 63% probability are expected to impact Cozumel and Costa Maya, likely affecting port operations and shore excursions at these locations. Roatan and Belize City will see elevated rain chances around 43% on Day 3, with overcast skies and southeast winds holding at 11 knots, which may affect shore excursion timing and embarkation procedures. Beyond 48 hours, persistent overcast conditions combined with continued southeast winds near 11 knots and a 43% rain probability suggest that cruise operations in Roatan and Belize City should prepare for possible weather-related delays. Passengers and crew should monitor updates for evolving rain impacts in these ports.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today, elevated east-southeast winds at 18 to 20 knots will affect Aruba, Curacao, and Bonaire, with clear skies and zero percent rain chance supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions despite the 20-knot wind speed. Port operations in Cartagena will also observe similar east winds near 20 knots, requiring attention to mooring and gangway safety. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, sustained east winds at 20 knots with drizzle in Aruba and partly cloudy skies in Curacao and Bonaire will continue, maintaining Small Craft Advisory conditions but with no rain impact as probabilities remain at 0 percent. By day three, expect slightly lighter east winds near 19 knots with overcast skies and a 4 percent rain chance in Aruba, indicating stable conditions for continued port and ship operations across the Southern Caribbean. Passengers and crew should remain aware that elevated steady winds will persist through the period, necessitating vigilance during all dock and tender activities in all ports.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today, easterly winds at 9 knots and partly cloudy skies prevail around Barbados with a 30% chance of rain that may affect shore excursions and embarkation activities. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, east-northeast winds increase to 10-12 knots with drizzle and a 35% rain chance expected to impact Barbados, St. Lucia, and Martinique port operations and tendering. By day three, steady ENE winds at 10 knots will bring overcast skies with a reduced 16% rain probability, likely improving conditions for Antigua, St. Kitts, and St. Maarten. Passengers and crew should anticipate generally manageable sea states but remain alert for light precipitation affecting outdoor activities in the region through day two. No marine wind warnings are in effect at this time.
regions.los-angeles: Today in the Los Angeles region including Los Angeles / San Pedro, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island, winds are light from the WSW at 3 knots with clear skies and a 0% chance of rain, providing ideal conditions for smooth embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, sustained WSW winds will increase to 11 knots with partly cloudy skies today and overcast conditions tomorrow, accompanied by a 2% rain chance that does not impact port or cruise operations at any location. Ports of call including Los Angeles / San Pedro and Long Beach will experience steady WSW winds at 11 knots through this period, supporting normal vessel maneuvering and dockside activity. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift to the south at 8 knots with partly cloudy conditions and zero rain probability, maintaining favorable conditions for ongoing cruise operations and passenger activities at all regional ports. Overall, no marine wind warnings or weather hazards are forecast through the next three days in this area.
regions.ensenada: Today, winds are light from the east-northeast at 4 knots with clear skies and zero percent rain chance, providing ideal conditions for embarkation and shore excursions at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds to shift to west-northwest at 15 knots with partly cloudy skies and no rain, which will primarily influence operations at Ensenada port with slightly increased breeze but no impact on outdoor activities. By day three, winds will decrease to 7 knots from the west-southwest with partly cloudy conditions and a 1 percent chance of rain, maintaining smooth sailing and shore excursion conditions across all three ports. No significant weather changes are developing beyond 48 hours, with light westerly winds and minimal rain probability continuing to support safe and efficient cruise operations. Passengers and crew can plan confidently for port visits with consistent visibility and calm seas expected throughout the period.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today in Cabo San Lucas, winds are light from the NNW at 3 knots with clear skies and a 0% chance of rain, supporting smooth port operations and shore excursions in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect W winds at 9 knots with a 4% rain chance and isolated drizzle today, and by tomorrow, overcast skies with a 10% rain chance, which may affect shore excursions primarily in San Jose del Cabo and La Paz due to increased cloud cover. Winds will remain steady from the west at 9 knots through this period, maintaining manageable conditions for embarkation and disembarkation across all three ports. Beyond 48 hours, overcast conditions persist with a 0% rain chance and W winds steady at 9 knots, indicating continued stable conditions for cruise operations without rain-related impacts. Passengers and crew should anticipate mostly overcast skies but minimal weather disruptions as the cruise progresses through this region.
regions.mazatlan: Today in the Mazatlan region, winds are light from the NNE at 2 knots with clear skies and zero percent rain chance, providing ideal conditions for smooth embarkation and shore excursions at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata. Beyond 48 hours, winds shift NW at 10 knots with drizzle, though rain probability remains at 0 percent, so drizzle is not expected to impact disembarkation or shore excursions in Altata. The evolving wind directions and overcast skies will require crew to monitor sea conditions carefully for passenger comfort but do not pose operational hazards at this time. Passengers should plan accordingly for overcast conditions and light drizzle beyond day two while remaining confident in uninterrupted port access.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today in Puerto Vallarta, we have light east-northeast winds at 2 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 0% chance of rain, so port operations and shore excursions at Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala are proceeding without weather-related delays. Beyond 48 hours, winds remain steady from the southwest near 11 knots with continued overcast skies and zero rain probability, maintaining smooth cruise ship operations and safe shore excursions throughout the region. No marine wind warnings or precipitation concerns are forecasted at any port during this period. Passengers and crew can plan outdoor activities with confidence given these calm wind speeds and dry conditions.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today, winds out of the west at 5 knots and clear skies with zero percent rain probability are providing excellent conditions for port operations and shore excursions in Barcelona, Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, Ibiza, Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar. Beyond 48 hours, a 61% rain probability with north winds at 5 knots will continue to affect Marseille, Nice, and Corsica, maintaining wet conditions and possible operational adjustments in these key ports. regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today, winds are light from the SSW at 2 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 0% chance of rain in Athens/Piraeus, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions with no weather-related delays expected. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, the region will see mostly overcast skies and winds from the south to SSW at 6 to 7 knots, with rain probabilities remaining low at 3-5% in Athens and similar conditions expected in Santorini and Mykonos. On day three, an elevated 40% rain chance may affect port operations and shore excursions in Athens, Santorini, and Rhodes as winds shift to SSE at 7 knots under persistent overcast skies.
